entry.title: How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP
entry.content:
Terry Penner and Jürgen Butsmann discuss the latest developments in SAP Business AI for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).  The conversation focuses on how Artificial Intelligence is evolving at SAP, with particular attention to large language models (LLMs) and their growing application in SAP cloud ERP and SAP BTP.

Listen to the podcast: Spotify | Apple Podcasts

Topics discussed:

Part 1: Evolution of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

  • Latest developments in the AI market
  • SAP’s ERP strategy evolution for AI
  • Latest solution architecture updates

Part 2:  Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

  • What is Joule
  • Differentiating AI offerings
  • How can AI add value to a business
  • Partner role with SAP Business AI

Part 3: Cloud ERP AI Priorities

  • SAP cloud ERP AI development priorities
  • SAP’s Business AI scenario priorities

This blog is a follow-up to AI for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: deep-dive discussion

About the speakers:

Jürgen Butsmann is part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Marketing team responsible for AI in the context of SAP S/4HANA, finance and digital supply chain. Jürgen has been with SAP for more than 26 years.  He is a recognized expert for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP .

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP marketing team for Business Applications, focused on SAP BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 23 years of technical and business experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Part 1: Evolution of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Latest developments in the AI market

Terry: In today's discussion, we'll explore how Artificial Intelligence for SAP S/4HANA and the SAP Business Technology Platform is evolving. Jürgen, what new developments have occurred in the AI market since we last spoke?

Jürgen: The evolution of technology continues unabated. We're observing an increase in services such as document services and advanced large language models (LLMs). The LLMs we see today are notably more fact-based, not just designed for entertainment purposes like the early iterations of ChatGPT, but for fact-oriented applications like those seen with Aleph Alpha. Additionally, there have been multiple iterations and diverse models emerging tailored to specific needs. We've also noticed a decline in pricing, which makes these AI services increasingly attractive and accessible in the market.

Terry: Absolutely. We've also witnessed significant legal developments, with content owners like The New York Times and others looking to withdraw their material from large language models, underscoring the importance of having robust and compliant data sources for these models.

SAP’s ERP strategy evolution for AI

Terry: How has SAP's ERP strategy for AI evolved?

Jürgen: Our strategy has evolved significantly, placing a more substantial emphasis on an AI-first approach across SAP. This shift means a lot of new ideas and pre-made implementations for our customers. We've taken a two-pronged approach in our investments. First, we're enhancing end-user productivity by integrating digital assistants into our systems, with Joule being a primary component. Although still early in deployment, these tools signify a shift from traditional graphical user interfaces to more intuitive digital assistant interactions.

Further confirming this direction, our CEO, Christian Klein, anticipates that in ten years, most system users will interact with digital assistants rather than traditional interfaces. Our focus also extends to natural language interactions within these systems, fostering intelligent activities and responses.

 

Secondly, we are concentrating on enhancing end-to-end processes. While we continue to enhance user interaction and productivity on the front end, significant efforts are directed towards optimizing large-scale processes, particularly within enterprise service management. This holistic approach ensures not just improvements in individual functionalities but also the acceleration of end-to-end process flows, all underpinned by advanced AI and generative AI technologies. This dual focus aligns with our commitment to transforming business processes through innovative AI applications.

Latest solution architecture updates

Terry: What are the latest updates to SAP's AI solution architecture since then?

Jürgen: One major change is the ongoing development of Joule. Initially, Joule had a rather rigid architecture that was not very flexible and did not support LLMs. This has changed. We have introduced the capability to create prompts using LLM support, which is a priority that has helped increase acceptance of this digital system and co-pilot for everyday work. Additionally, we've improved context sensitivity—knowing where you are in your system and what steps you are processing. This is a key differentiator from other co-pilots on the market and allows us to integrate responses that feel less artificial and more contextually aware.  It's able to  integrate proprietary documents into the context of the business processes.

Furthermore, we continue to develop its extensibility. We've opened possibilities for customers to tailor the co-pilot to perform functions specific to their needs.

Terry: And regarding extensibility, that’s a significant area where SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) collaborates with the ERP team by supplying developer tools and experiences, such as the vector engine within SAP HANA, to support these functionalities—it's a joint effort.

Part 2:  Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

What is Joule?

Terry: We’ve spoken quite a bit about Joule. Could you clarify exactly what Joule is?

Jürgen: Essentially, Joule serves as an interface for natural language commands within our system architecture. It acts as an entry point where users can interact with the system to trigger functions or retrieve necessary business process information. While Joule leverages AI technologies, its use doesn’t alter the inherent capabilities of our AI. It fits seamlessly into our system, making those use cases distinct and noteworthy. Ideally, Joule simplifies user interactions by providing a natural, conversational interface with the system. While our systems could operate without Joule, using it enhances the user experience, making interactions more engaging and straightforward.

  

Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

Terry: What's the difference with SAP Business AI? How should customers differentiate potential AI offerings?

Jürgen: The top priority for SAP Business AI is to provide clear and measurable value to customers. Before using AI scenarios, there must be a reasonable and measurable return on investment. The focus is on implementing AI in SAP systems without the need to change generic functionality, where the system's context details can be used to address processes more effectively.

The reliability of AI use cases is a major focus, based on fact-based foundation models whenever possible. All AI-assisted processes undergo an ethical check and are supported by the data security of SAP Business AI architecture.

We are also continuing to refine our pricing approach and evaluating if we will continue using current AI units or shift to different technologies in the future. Customers consider usage, potential value add, costs, and investments when determining the break-even point for their use cases. Moreover, customers expect our AI solutions to be user-friendly.

Terry: Adding to that, not all AI use cases deliver sufficient value for application. It's crucial to concentrate on scenarios with high-quality underlying data linked to large language models. For example, one effective use of AI I've observed involves searching through support cases and providing recommendations to new team members, enhancing the search experience and improving how information is interpreted and suggested.

How can AI add value to a business

Terry: How would you explain to a customer how AI can add value to a business?

Jürgen: When considering how AI supports business processes, the user's connection to the system is paramount. Ideally, this happens through a digital co-pilot, like Joule in our case. Users interact with Joule by posing questions that prompt queries to a large language model, which in turn provides responses. However, large language models may lack specific business process details since those are unique and not universally predefined, leading to potentially incomplete answers. 

 

To address this, we might extend our architecture to incorporate calls to the ERP system or access other proprietary data, ensuring no sensitive business information is exposed to potentially vulnerable cloud environments. We balance accessibility with security by using a vector database to locate and integrate the right business-specific data from outside the large language model's scope.

As users submit human language requests, these are processed through the digital system and converted into technical prompts by a large language model. These prompts trigger the vector engine to retrieve the necessary information. The returning data can then be refined further by the large language models, which are all deployed securely within our system environment to prevent data leakage.

The data retrieval process benefits from enhancements such as semantic similarities and pattern recognition to continuously improve response accuracy. Once processed, the information is delivered back to the user, completing the cycle and delivering the value we aim for with our AI-enabled systems.

This systematic approach ensures that AI not only supports but also enhances business processes effectively while maintaining data integrity and security.

Terry: So, we could describe Joule as the front-end experience, distinct from the backend LLM that powers the AI functionalities.

Partner role with SAP Business AI

Terry: What role do partners play in this space?

Jürgen: Firstly, our partners have a deep understanding of our customers. It's crucial that partners are well-versed in our setup, strategy, architecture, pricing, and other elements that shape how we envision utilizing these services. We leverage our partners to create scalable solutions that precisely address specific customer needs. They are instrumental in implementing individual projects that tackle unique business challenges at the customer sites. Our partners have a proven track record of handling such use cases effectively.

Part 3: AI Extensibility and SAP BTP

SAP cloud ERP AI development priorities

Terry: What are some of the other AI development priorities at SAP cloud ERP, and how is SAP BTP supporting those AI scenarios?

 

Jürgen: We're integrating various technologies beyond what we've already discussed, like our work with live language models. For example, merging our capabilities with Microsoft Copilot offers expanded possibilities for enhancing natural language interactions. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and the AI Foundation are pivotal in anchoring these flexibilities. This integration allows us to broaden our scope and reinforce the support we provide for cloud ERP AI scenarios.

SAP’s Business AI scenario priorities

Terry: What business scenarios are central to SAP's Business AI focus?

Jürgen: Our focus isn't narrow, but we do see significant interest particularly in the finance sector. Currently, we're engaging extensively with businesses, customers, and partners to pinpoint key priorities specific to finance. Our approach begins by assessing what immediate actions we can take and then contemplating more visionary possibilities for the future. For example, one idea is the automatic creation of forecast scenarios based on historical data within our systems. This capability could enhance management, strategy, and decision-making meetings significantly, even without all finance experts gathered at one table. Additionally, there are numerous other aspects of finance where AI can play a transformative role.

Terry: We will link to some specific use cases within the podcast notes. Thank you, Jürgen, for joining us and sharing your insights into AI for cloud ERP and the business technology platform. It's been enlightening.

Jürgen: Thank you for having me. I hope our discussion helps people understand better where we are now and where we are headed with our technology.

[ Note:  This blog was adapted from the podcast transcript by an SAP system using Azure OpenAI–gpt-4 ]

Key links:

  • Four ways to infuse business-ready, trusted AI into your processes
  • SAP BTP for S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Business AI

Read more in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: IBM Granite.13b.chat Large Language Model- Now GA on the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
SAP’s generative AI Hub is the central place for BTP application developers to access and use generative AI tools and models, and developers can now choose IBM Granite as their large language model (LLM). This article will explain some of Granite’s unique characteristics and then show you how to access and use it.

IBM Granite.13b.chat is a decoder, English-only, enterprise-grade LLM and part of a series of IBM foundation models for generative artificial intelligence tasks. IBM stands behind Granite LLMs with IP indemnification protection according to its Standard Terms and Conditions, to provide users with peace of mind. Granite.13b.chat was trained on enterprise-relevant content that meets rigorous data governance, regulatory, and risk criteria defined and enforced by the IBM AI Ethics Code and Chief Privacy Office. You can read the details here: https://www.ibm.com/downloads/cas/X9W4O6BM. Granite.13b.chat delivers high accuracy for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), summarization, content generation, named entity recognition, insight extraction, and classification.

A considerable portion of Granite training data is from business domains. For business-specific tasks in domains such as finance, some IBM performance benchmarks suggest that the accuracy of Granite models is on par with larger models (70B parameter+) at lower latency and, due to its smaller size (13B parameter), lower infrastructure spending.

AI capabilities in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad that utilize the generative AI hub can leverage Granite.13b.chat.

Get started by:

  1. Learning how to use foundational models on SAP AI Core (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-consumption-llm.html), and
  2. learning how to prompt Granite.13b.chat (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/docs/content/wsj/analyze-data/fm-models-ibm-chat.html?context=wx&audience=wdp).

For more information about Granite.13b.chat and instruction-tuning details, see the model card (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/login?context=wx&sign_in_target=%2Fwx%2Fsamples%2Fmodels%2Fibm%2Fgranite-13b-chat-v2%3Fcontext%3Dwx%3Fcontext%3Dwx%26audience%3Dwdp).

entry.title: SAP BTP: The Foundation for Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The digital revolution is in full swing, and at its core lies the transformative power of artificial intelligence (AI). For the tech-savvy enterprise, AI is not just a buzzword, but a strategic imperative. SAP Business AI emerges as a key enabler of this transformation, offering a robust suite of tools that empower businesses to optimize operations, automate processes, and gain a competitive edge.

SAP: A Legacy of Innovation in the Enterprise Landscape

Since its inception in 1972, SAP has been a pioneer in enterprise technology, guiding businesses through technological shifts and helping them leverage new opportunities. With SAP Business AI, SAP continue this tradition, delivering a cutting-edge platform that harnesses the power of AI to redefine business processes.

SAP Business AI: More Than Just Automation

SAP Business AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses leverage data and technology. It's not just about automating tasks; it's about fundamentally changing how businesses operate and innovate. Think of it as an intelligent layer embedded within your existing SAP landscape, constantly learning and adapting to optimize processes and drive better outcomes.

Key Components and Applications: A Technical Perspective

SAP Business AI is deeply integrated across SAP's entire product portfolio, providing seamless access to AI capabilities within the context of everyday business processes. Here's a closer look at the technical components that make it so powerful:

Across key areas like Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Procurement, Marketing & Commerce, Sales & Services, and IT. This broad reach allows businesses to leverage AI in every aspect of their operations.

SAP Business AI provides a dual approach to AI, offering both pre-built AI services and the tools to build custom AI solutions. For specific business processes, SAP offers ready-to-use AI services that can be easily adapted to unique workflows. These services cover areas such as document processing, data attribute recommendations, and Robotic Process Automation.

At the heart of SAP Business AI lies a robust data foundation. This foundation includes SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as the underlying infrastructure, along with services like SAP Datasphere, Analytics Cloud, and Integration Suite. These tools make it easy to connect, model, and analyze data across different applications, ensuring that AI algorithms have access to the high-quality data they need.

SAP also recognizes the importance of collaboration in the AI ecosystem. Establishing strategic partnerships with other industry leaders, such as Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM, to leverage their expertise and technologies. This collaborative approach ensures that SAP Business AI remains at the forefront of AI innovation, so that with leading AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers, giving customers access to the latest innovations and ensuring flexibility in their AI deployments.

SAP Business AI Approach: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible

SAP's approach to AI is guided by three core principles, as highlighted in the image: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible.

Relevant: SAP Business AI is designed to be relevant from day one. Its deep integration with SAP applications ensures that AI capabilities are readily available and easy to use within the context of everyday business processes.

Reliable: SAP has a long-standing reputation for delivering reliable enterprise software, and this commitment extends to SAP Business AI. The platform is built on a foundation of high-quality data, rigorous testing, and robust security measures, ensuring that businesses can trust its accuracy and performance.

Responsible: SAP recognizes the ethical implications of AI and is committed to developing and deploying AI in a responsible manner. This includes addressing concerns around bias, discrimination, and privacy, and ensuring that AI is used to augment human capabilities, not replace them.

Assessing Business AI Infused Across the SAP Product Portfolio

AI is already deeply ingrained in SAP's enterprise applications and business technology, used by tens of thousands of their cloud customers. AI-powered capabilities, based on proprietary deep learning and machine learning algorithms, are driving significant business value across SAP's cloud solutions. These capabilities include intelligent document processing, personalized recommendations, natural language processing, forecasting, and more. This means that businesses can leverage AI capabilities within their existing SAP solutions, without having to implement separate AI tools or platforms.

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

Transforming Business Applications with Generative AI

Generative AI is ushering in a new era of possibilities for business software. It enables the creation of new content, summarization of complex information, automated code writing, and much more. This new generation of AI can reason through business problems and offer solutions that were previously unimaginable.
SAP customers can expect to see tangible value from generative AI use cases across the entire SAP portfolio: Generative AI at SAP 

 Let's explore how generative AI is transforming key areas within the SAP ecosystem:

  • Cloud ERP: Streamlining processes like the verification of delivery notes, automating document processing with greater accuracy and efficiency. For example, imagine automatically processing thousands of invoices with minimal human intervention, using AI to extract relevant data and match it with purchase orders.
  • Human Capital Management: Freeing up managers' time by automating tasks like job postings, candidate sourcing, and performance reviews, while also reducing bias in these processes. AI can help identify the best candidates for a job by analyzing resumes and social media profiles, personalize onboarding experiences by providing tailored content and recommendations, and even predict employee attrition by identifying patterns in employee data.
  • Spend Management & Business Network: Empowering category managers with the information they need to make strategic procurement decisions, leveraging market dynamics and cost structure insights. AI can analyze spending patterns, identify cost-saving opportunities, and optimize supplier relationships by analyzing market data and predicting price fluctuations.
  • Customer Relationship Management: Elevating customer engagement by providing sales teams with personalized insights into customer preferences and purchase history, leading to tailored interactions and improved customer satisfaction. Imagine AI-powered chatbots that provide instant support and personalized recommendations to customers, improving customer satisfaction and reducing support costs. AI can also help sales teams prioritize leads, personalize marketing campaigns, and identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Simplifying application development by enabling AI-powered code generation and test script creation, allowing developers to focus on more complex tasks. This can significantly reduce development time and costs, allowing businesses to innovate faster. Tools like SAP Build Code help automate code generation and testing, reducing development time and costs. For example, a developer could describe a desired functionality in natural language, and SAP Build Code would generate the corresponding code automatically.

Recognizing the Integration of SAP BTP with Business AI

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the underlying platform for SAP Business AI, providing the infrastructure and tools needed to build and deploy AI-powered applications.

Here are some build-in components of SAP BTP that are particularly relevant for AI development:

  • SAP Build Code: This tool empowers developers to build applications faster with the help of generative AI. The Joule copilot provides intelligent code suggestions and automates repetitive coding tasks, increasing developer productivity. For example, Joule can automatically generate code for common tasks, such as creating user interfaces or connecting to databases.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: This no-code solution enables business users to automate workflows and processes without writing any code. Its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and AI capabilities make it easy to create and deploy automations. This empowers business users to automate tasks without relying on IT, increasing agility and efficiency.
  • SAP HANA Cloud: This powerful in-memory database is optimized for handling large volumes of data and complex analytical queries, making it ideal for AI applications. Its ability to process data in memory allows for real-time analysis and insights, which is crucial for many AI use cases.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: This cloud-based analytics platform provides a comprehensive suite of tools for data visualization, analysis, and planning. The integration of Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot, further enhances the platform's capabilities, enabling users to gain deeper insights from their data. For example, users can ask Joule to "show me the top 10 customers by revenue" or "predict next quarter's sales".

Focusing on the future of AI in the enterprise

SAP Business AI is more than just a technology; it's a strategic enabler for the modern enterprise. By seamlessly integrating AI across its product portfolio, SAP is empowering businesses to optimize processes, automate tasks, gain valuable insights, and ultimately achieve better outcomes. With a strong commitment to relevance, reliability, and responsibility, SAP Business AI is paving the way for a future where businesses can harness the full potential of AI in an ethical and sustainable manner.

Ready to delve deeper into the world of SAP Business AI? Here are some resources to get you started:

  • Artificial­ Intelligence­ |­ SAP ­Business ­AI
  • AI Solutions on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • AI Ethics | SAP Global AI Ethics Policy
  • SAP ­Community:­ Artificial­ Intelligence­ and­ Machine­ Learning­ at­ SAP
  • SAP ­Learning:­ Tutorials­ for­ SAP ­Developers­
  • Discover SAP Business AI features
  • Implement your use cases on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Learning: how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 4 - Integration via Action
entry.content:
In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup
  • Part 2: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Javascript function
  • Part 3: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via REST API
  • Part 4: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Action (current blog)

SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Action

Currently, the recommended solution for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application is to use the new feature Action Integration. In this way, a SAP Build Action project (https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-actions-project ) for SAP AI Core service will be created first, and then integrated into your SAP Build Apps application.

1. Create an action in Lobby. and select "Build from Scratch." 

2. Add a custom action, select the operation as "POST," and provide the value of the endpoint according to your deployment of the AI core service, which you have setup in Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup

3. Add fields to Input Parameter and Body according the requirement of your SAP AI Core service instance

For examples:

sample JSON for input where you can get from https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/prompt-examples 

 

{
"messages": [
	{
		"role": "user",
		"content": "Your task is to generate a short summary of ..."
	}
],
"max_tokens": 100,
"temperature": 0.0,
"frequency_penalty": 0,
"presence_penalty": 0,
"stop": "null"
}

 

4. Test your SAP Build Action project

To test your SAP Build Action project, you need to add the BTP destination you created at Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup to SAP Build Control Tower. Still remember we set

 

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

 

in Part 1. This property makes the destination discoverable in the SAP Build Control Tower.

To understand more about how to manage destinations in Control Tower, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/manage-destinations

After adding the BTP destination, you can select it in your Action project and test it.

Once the test is successful, you can open the API response body and click "Generate Output" to populate your Action Output structure automatically. For more details, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/testing-actions Now, you have completed the creation of an SAP Build Action to consume the SAP AI Core service with just a copy and paste and a few mouse clicks.

5. Release and publish your action

6. Open your SAP Build Apps project, navigate to the Data tab, click "Add Integration," then select the "SAP Build Library" tile, browse, select the action you just published, and enable it in your SAP Build Apps project.

7. Switch back to "UI Canvas" tab, click the UI component, where you can want to trigger the action, open "Logic Canvas", drag and drop "Tigger Action" flow function to logic canvas and configure it

8. Please make sure to specify the correct BTP destination that you used when you created your action.

9. Configure the data binding for input and output of "Trigger Action" flow function

10. Preview and deploy your app on both web and mobile

To summarize what we have done, without writing a single line of code, just copy and paste, drag and drop, and a few mouse clicks, we created an SAP Build Action to consume SAP AI Core services, integrated it into SAP Build Apps, and developed an application with AI capabilities.

Compared to Part 2: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Javascript function where you need to write JavaScript code, and  Part 3: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via REST API where you need to manually configure the resource schema for the REST API, the new solution with Action Integration offers a much better experience.

In future blogs, I will further discuss how to use Action Integration to enable more AI capabilities, such as RAG (Retrieval-augment generation), image analysis, anomaly detection, voice-to-text, etc. I will also write other blogs on how to use Action Integration to perform more complex data transactions, such as OData linking, deep inserts, etc. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Suncor Energy derives business value through SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The SAP MaxAttention CoE NA Team has been recognized as a prestigious SAP Innovation Awards 2024 Finalist! This accolade celebrates our innovative work with SUNCOR ENERGY, leveraging SAP Business AI to drive significant advancements. Our collaboration with Suncor exemplifies the power of cutting-edge technology in transforming business operations. Through SAP Business AI, we have delivered intelligent solutions that enhance efficiency, optimize processes, and foster sustainable growth. CIO Magazine has published an article featuring this success story.

Business Challenges

Suncor Energy has a legal and regulatory obligation to charge tax on intercompany transactions. Currently, the intercompany journal entries do not have tax amounts. The challenge is to compute the tax amount based on tax code and/or tax jurisdiction and post the journal entry with the consolidated tax amount. The solution should reduce the risks associated with regulatory filing, minimize manual effort, and shorten the period-end closing time.

Solution

As part of its monthly closing process, Suncor needed to reconcile inter-company tax postings for journal entries, which involved significant manual effort. To streamline this task, Suncor collaborated with the SAP MaxAttention team to develop an automation solution. Within less than four weeks, a prototype was delivered, followed by a successful production deployment in under six weeks. A key highlight of the solution was its ability to leverage Suncor’s existing SAP investments, enhancing and optimizing the process to meet evolving business challenges with rapid time-to-value. The hyper-automation solution utilized SAP Business AI, SAP Build Process Automation, S/4HANA REST APIs, Fiori UI, and SAP HANA Cloud, resulting in a reduced TCO and improved ROI.

Outcomes: 

  • Transaction Volume – Savings of $1.176 Million Interest and Fines Exposure (annually)
  • 40 pairings with over 5K customer line items per execution
  • ​​The Inter-company Tax Automation project helped Suncor reduce the risks associated with regulatory filing, minimize manual effort, and shorten the period-end closing time.
  • Suncor end users are happy with the solution, and the adoption is very high due to the reduction in tedious manual intervention and data processing; end users are now able to focus on strategic tasks rather than mundane data validation activities.

For further details, please refer to: https://www.cio.com/article/3544650/suncor-energy-derives-business-value-through-business-ai-and-automation.html 

Contact Person:  Rohit Dwivedi, Kiran Kola

entry.title: How Generative AI Can Assist in SAP Fiori Applications: A Practical Demonstration
entry.content:
What if your SAP Fiori applications could anticipate your needs and provide intelligent suggestions to streamline your workflow? With the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence, integrating generative AI models into enterprise applications is no longer a distant future but a present opportunity to enhance efficiency and decision-making.

In this post, I'll share a practical demonstration of how I integrated a generative AI model into an SAP Fiori application using OpenUI5. This solution enhances the creation of maintenance orders by providing AI-generated suggestions based on the order's title and description, helping users avoid overlooking critical steps.

The Solution

I developed a frontend application using OpenUI5, hosted on Vercel, that mirrors a maintenance order request interface in SAP Fiori. The key enhancement is the addition of AI-generated suggestions that appear as the user inputs the maintenance order's title and description.

You can test the live application yourself here: https://fiori-app-form.vercel.app/ 

 

 These suggestions offer important steps for performing the task, effectively serving as a smart checklist to assist users in making informed decisions.

Technical Details

Development Environment

  • Frontend: Built with OpenUI5 and deployed on the Vercel platform.
  • Backend: A locally running API utilizing a generative AI model (LLM) to process user inputs and generate suggestions.

Integration with AI

The integration with the generative AI model is achieved through HTTP requests to the local API. When the user enters the maintenance order's title and description, this data is sent to the API, which processes it and returns a list of suggested steps.

 

 

 

 

.route('/infer', methods=['POST'])
def infer():
    """Endpoint para gerar conteúdo com a API do Google."""
    data = request.get_json(force=True)

    if not data or 'prompt' not in data:
        return jsonify({'error': 'No prompt provided'}), 400

    try:
        prompt_parts = [data['prompt']]
        response = model.generate_content(prompt_parts)
        output_text = response.text

    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Erro na inferência: {e}")
        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500

    return jsonify({'output_text': output_text})

 

 

 

Interface Implementation

The user interface is crafted to be intuitive and familiar for SAP Fiori users, which helps to reduce the learning curve. Standard OpenUI5 components are utilized to gather user input and present AI-generated suggestions.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

<!-- Botão "Gerar Sugestões" -->
                <Button id="generateSuggestionsButton" text="Gerar Sugestões" press="onGenerateSuggestions" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />

                <!-- Lista de Passos Sugeridos -->
                <Label text="Passos Sugeridos" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />
                <List id="suggestionsList" width="80%" items="{suggestionsModel>/suggestions}">
                    <StandardListItem title="{suggestionsModel>}" />
                </List>

 

 

 

Demonstration in Action

Here's how the application works in practice:

  1. Title: "Air Filter Replacement"
  2. Description: "The air filter of the compressor needs to be replaced due to particle buildup."

Upon entering this information, the user receives immediate suggestions:

  • 1. Turn off the compressor and disconnect it from the electrical power.
  • 2. Locate the air filter, which is usually in a plastic or metal case.
  • 3. Loosen the screws or clips securing the air filter box.
  • 4. Remove the old air filter and inspect it for dirt and damage.
  • 5. Insert the new air filter into the housing and secure it with the screws or latches.
  • 6. Replace the air filter box on the compressor.
  • 7. Turn on the compressor and check for air leaks.
     
     

You can test the live application yourself here: https://fiori-app-form.vercel.app/ 

This practical demonstration highlights the potential of integrating generative AI models into SAP Fiori applications. While this example operates locally without OData communication, it showcases how AI can be leveraged to improve daily tasks and workflows.

 

I'm excited to hear your thoughts! Share your experiences, ideas, and questions in the comments.

Thank you for reading! If you're interested in more technical details or have any questions, feel free to reach out.

SAP Fiori SAP AI Core SAP AI Launchpad  Artificial Intelligence SAP Maintenance Assistant mobile app

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2411 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2411 builds on our strategic investment in generative AI for field service organizations, now with extended multilingual AI support. In addition, we introduced several new features designed to simplify user workflows, strengthen integration, and increase functionality. In this blog, you can find an overview of the most notable features, along with a demo video showcasing key highlights. For a complete list of all the features in the 2411 release, click here. In this blog, I will highlight the key features that will help enhance how you manage field services, including:

  • Generative AI: Multilingual support (French and German) for the Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering.
  • User Experience enhancements: New Morning Horizon theme, Business Partner enhancements, manage work time in the Time & Material Journal, extended sort and filter capabilities, extended customization capabilities, and more.
  • Service Map: Visualize activity details on hover, and a simplified view of travel time/distance.
  • Integration: Enhanced SAP S/4HANA Service integration, New Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities, Production Order integration, and item-based accounting for an unplanned service.
  • AI-based scheduling improvements: Simulate the effect of adjustments in auto-scheduling policies, and recalculate travel time for bookings.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive. 

Generative AI

SAP Field Service Management Generative AI services are now available in French and German, enabling more users to access Gen AI features in their native language. In addition to simplifying workflows, generative AI in SAP Field Service Management makes it easier to unlock and benefit from valuable insights that were previously difficult to access. These enhanced workflows and actionable insights provide substantial value to field service organizations. According to SAP customer benchmarking, by utilizing these features, field service organizations can achieve (on average):

  • ⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity.
  • ⬆️ 5% improvement in first-time fix rates.
  • ⬇️ 15% reduction in time to review and plan service requests.

Take a look at the demo video below showcasing our AI highlights in 2411:


AI Assisted Services - Now Available in German and French

AI Assisted Services (German 🇩🇪)

AI Services in German - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary

 
AI Assisted Services (French 🇫🇷) 

AI Services in French - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary
➡️ For more information on AI services such as Intelligent Filtering, Equipment Insights, and the Activity Summary, please refer to the 2408 Release Blog. These translations are made possible using SAP Translation Hub.

AI-based Scheduling Improvements:

Simulate the Effect of Adjustments in Custom Auto-scheduling Policies

The new Policy Simulation feature allows users to test scheduling policy changes in a controlled environment, ensuring no impact on live schedules. This enhancement allows users to configure and evaluate adjustments, such as prioritizing skills levels or enforcing objectives, in a secure environment. By simulating outcomes, organizations can safely refine strategies, and ensure alignment with evolving operating conditions. Learn more about this innovation here.

Recalculate Travel Time for Bookings

Thanks to feedback from our Customer Influence Program, accurate travel times are now easier to achieve via automatic travel time recalculation. Travel times are updated automatically when planning or replanning activities, ensuring dispatchers always have precise data. This enhancement helps reduce manual adjustments, minimizes dispatcher confusion, and improves overall scheduling accuracy. Learn more about this feature here.


User Experience & Usability Enhancements

SAP Field Service Management 2411 also features several key user experience and usability highlights, which are summarized below and in the demo video:


Morning Horizon Theme

The new Morning Horizon theme in SAP Field Service Management introduces a fresh, modern design, providing users with a consistent and visually appealing interface. This standard theme enhances the user experience with improved aesthetics, accessibility, and a cohesive look and feel across all SAP applications. Learn more here.

 

SAP Feedback Survey

This provides an easy way to contribute feedback on product functionality, helping SAP Field Service Management refine and enhance its offerings based on real-world usage and customer needs. Learn more about participating here.


Dispatching Board

Configurable Header Information in the Modal UI (preview)

The new header configuration in SAP Field Service Management allows organizations to customize the key details displayed in the Modal UI for Service Calls and Activities via screen configurations. This enhancement makes it easier to display critical information by tailoring screens to meet your company’s unique requirements. Learn more about this feature here.



Manage Approvals for Worktime in the Time and Material Journal

The enhanced work time management capabilities in SAP Field Service Management improve tracking and approval of time entries through the Time and Material (T&M) Journal. This update harmonizes processes in the T&M Journal and improves workflows for work time approvals. Learn more about this feature here.

 
Option to Display the Activity Cancellation Reason 

Users can now benefit from increased transparency for cancelled activities in SAP Field Service Management, with clear visibility into the reasons for activity cancellations. For more information, visit the product documentation.


Service Call Extension Tab in the Modal UI

Extensions in SAP Field Service Management now provide greater flexibility for placement in desired outlets. The new Service Call extension tab in the Modal UI allows users to position extensions in more key locations, ensuring they are easily accessible and aligned with operational priorities. For more details, click here.

Sample Extension

 
Option to Save Column Layouts in all List Views (Preview)

The 2411 release provides enhanced flexibility for customizing column settings and layouts of the activity list. Users can easily configure visible fields and group data to align with specific operational needs and tactical scheduling scenarios, creating a more intuitive user experience with faster access to relevant data. For further info, see here.

 
Enhancements for List Views

This release introduces improved filtering capabilities, offering enhanced options for managing list views. Users can now easily filter by key information when working with a list view in the planning list, making this workflow faster and more efficient. More information, here.


Service Map

Quick Access to Activity Details

Dispatchers can now view essential activity information from the Service Map by simply hovering over an activity, reducing the number of clicks. This popup allows dispatchers to instantly view key details, and can be easily configured to display the companies specific requirements. For more details, please see here.


Service Map – Close/Dock Time-Distance Popup Box

When using the Service Map, dispatchers now can easily view detailed travel information directly on the Service Map with a simple pop-up box. This pop-up displays both travel time and distance, and can be opened, moved, and closed as needed, allowing dispatchers to quickly access and manage information without cluttering the screen. More details here.


Integration Improvements

New Fiori App for Viewing SAP Field Service Management Activities

The new SAP S/4HANA Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities provides a simplified and intuitive way to access activity details from SAP Field Service Management. With an organized data presentation, users can quickly navigate through activities and view critical information such as status, technician assignments, and partner data, directly from SAP S/4HANA. Get the full details here.


Cancellation of Activity from SAP S/4HANA

In SAP S/4HANA, SAP FSM activities can now be cancelled with detailed reasons, while seamlessly integrated into SAP Field Service Management. This ensures consistent data and improved transparency across both systems. For more details, refer to the help documentation.


Item-based Accounting for Unplanned Service

Item-based accounting and revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Service now integrates with SAP Field Service Management to support unplanned services. This enhancement ensures that revenue is accurately tracked and recognized based on actual service execution, bridging financial, profitability, and operational data for improved transparency and compliance. See the support documentation here.


Integration of Production Orders as Service Calls

Production Order integration with SAP Field Service Management enhances the connection between SAP Field Service Management and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC. This integration enables scheduling, execution, and confirmation of production order operations, with support for time effort, materials management, and more. More information here.

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2411 enhances the field service value chain with multilingual generative AI, streamlined workflows, and improved integration capabilities. We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help us shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned for the 2502 release, where even more innovations are on the way!

Get in touch!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which AI innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2411 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q4 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance we have added new product pages.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add three new product pages that consolidate links to all available release communication channels, like schedules, pre- and post-release communication, blogs, and more.

The new pages are:

  1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  2. SAP Mobile Services
  3. SAP Translation Hub

Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, documentation on releases and available modules, the GitHub Kyma repositories, as well as a helpful learning journey and hands-on tutorials.

Check it out.

Fig 1. The new page for SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

 

2. SAP Mobile Services

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 2. The new page for SAP Mobile Services.

 

3. SAP Translation Hub

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 3. The new page for SAP Translation Hub.

 

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 30 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

 

Please note that the product page for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud has been retired as of this update. For more information, you can refer to this blog post.

 

For centralized information on when SAP BTP products and services release new features refer to the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP.

Regarding Release Navigator updates, you can refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer:  The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.

Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

 

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Infusing AI into Journal Entry Process - Beyond Automation
entry.content:
Background:

During critical periods such as month-end, quarter-end, or year-end close, organizations often face a high volume of manual journal entries being posted into their S/4  systems. These entries can sometimes contain errors, especially when they are entered by various users who might not have a consistent understanding of the required formats, values, or related documentation.

Currently, these manual entries are posted directly into the system without prior checks, leading to potential discrepancies, accounting errors, or compliance violations. These errors could include incorrect amounts, incorrect GL codes, misaligned descriptions, missing or inaccurate supporting documentation, and others. To mitigate this risk, a more streamlined and controlled process is needed where the journal entries are parked first, reviewed for validation, and then posted after being confirmed as correct.

Key Issues:

  1. Manual Journal Entry Errors: These include issues such as:
    • Incorrect amounts posted for a particular entry type (e.g., a cost that is typically $5,000 being entered as $500).
    • Incorrect General Ledger (GL) codes or descriptions (e.g., the GL code does not match the standard or policy documentation).
    • Missing or incorrect attachment references (e.g., an invoice or supporting document is either not attached or is attached to the wrong entry).
    • Missing or incomplete information (e.g., missing approval, insufficient details for audit).
  2. Lack of Validation Prior to Posting: Since the journal entries are directly posted into the system, there is no opportunity to detect these errors before they are committed to the books, leading to potential audit issues, compliance risks, and manual corrections that are costly and time-consuming.

Solution Objective: To streamline this process, the goal is to implement a system where all manual journal entries are first "parked" (held temporarily) in the system, rather than posted immediately. During this parked state, the entries can undergo a series of automated checks or validations, leveraging historical data, company policy documents, and attachments to ensure accuracy and compliance. The system can then generate alerts or notifications to flag any anomalies, providing users with the opportunity to correct them before final posting.

Key Components: A Symphony of Technology

SAP BTP AI Services

Leverage pre-built AI models for document analysis, text extraction, and machine learning. These services enable intelligent data processing and automation.

S/4HANA

The core ERP system provides access to real-time financial data, enabling accurate and efficient journal entry creation and posting.

Data Pipelines

Securely transport data between BTP AI and S/4HANA, ensuring smooth and reliable integration of processed data for journal entries.

AI-Enabled Validation System:

An AI-enabled solution would help in detecting these anomalies by analyzing historical data, policy documents, and attachment details. The AI system would focus on the following validation areas:

  1. Amount Validation:
    • Historical Amount Comparison: AI can learn typical amounts for certain types of journal entries based on historical data. For example, if a specific type of entry (e.g., payroll) typically has an amount in the range of $4,500 to $5,500, the system can flag entries that are too high or too low.
    • Amount Consistency: Cross-checking the amounts with similar entries from previous periods or other business units to ensure consistency.
  2. GL Code Validation:
    • GL Code Matching: Ensuring that the correct GL code is used based on the entry type. If an entry for "supplies" is posted under a GL code used for "payroll", it can be flagged.
    • GL Code Description Match: The AI can validate that the description associated with the GL code aligns with the standard naming convention or description used in the policy documents.
    • Cross-checking with Financial Policies: Validation against internal financial policies or specific rules regarding what types of entries can use which GL codes.
  3. Description Validation:
    • Attachment Match: The AI can compare the GL description with the content of the attached document to ensure they are consistent. For example, if the entry refers to "Consulting Fees" but the attachment shows "Travel Expenses", the system can flag this discrepancy.
    • Standard Description Check: Ensuring that entries match the standard descriptions specified in internal accounting guidelines or corporate documentation.
  4. Attachment Validation:
    • Document Existence Check: Ensuring that required documents (e.g., invoices, contracts, receipts) are attached. If they are missing, an alert is generated.
    • Attachment Type Matching: The system can check whether the correct type of attachment is associated with the entry. For example, if the entry refers to a "Vendor Payment," the attached document can be an invoice or a vendor agreement.
    • Document Consistency: Comparing the details in the attachment (e.g., amounts, dates) with the amounts or other details in the journal entry to verify consistency.
  5. Approval Workflow Validation:
    • Approval Matching: Ensuring that all journal entries have been approved according to the organization’s internal controls. The AI can validate whether the appropriate manager or department head has provided the necessary approval for the entry type.
    • Approval Policy Check: Cross-checking entries against approval thresholds defined by the organization (e.g., entries above $10,000 must have senior management approval).
  6. Data Completeness:
    • Missing Information: Identifying any missing or incomplete fields in the journal entry. For example, ensuring that the "reason for entry" field or "cost center" field is populated.
    • Consistency with Historical Data: Cross-checking the current entry against similar entries in historical data to ensure that the same fields are populated appropriately.
  7. Audit & Compliance Validation:
    • Regulatory Compliance: Validating that the journal entry complies with relevant accounting regulations (e.g., GAAP, IFRS). The AI system can cross-check entries for common non-compliance patterns.
    • Audit Trail: Ensuring that there is a clear and complete audit trail for all parked journal entries, including details on who made the entry, when it was parked, and who approved it.
 

 

 

Solution Approach

  1. AI and Machine Learning Models:
    • Use machine learning algorithms that can be trained on historical data, previous journal entries, and standard accounting practices to recognize patterns of typical errors and anomalies.
    • The system can apply supervised learning techniques, using labeled data (e.g., previous correct and incorrect journal entries), to improve accuracy over time.
  2. Natural Language Processing (NLP):
    • To validate descriptions, attachment content, and other free-form text fields, NLP techniques can be applied to identify mismatches between the journal entry and the attached documentation.
  3. Integration with S/4  Systems:
    • The solution can seamlessly integrate with existing S/4  or accounting systems to "park" the journal entries and raise alerts automatically when discrepancies are found.
  4. Automated Alerts and Notifications:
    • Once anomalies are detected, the system can send automated alerts to relevant personnel (accountants, managers, auditors) with clear descriptions of the issue and suggested corrections.

Key Benefits

  • Improved Accuracy: By automating the validation of manual entries before posting, the AI system can significantly reduce the likelihood of errors.
  • Cost and Time Savings: Automating the review and validation process saves time and money that would otherwise be spent on manual corrections and audits.
  • Compliance and Risk Mitigation: Ensures that all entries comply with financial regulations and internal policies, reducing the risk of non-compliance and potential penalties.
  • Audit Readiness: The system maintains a comprehensive log of all validation checks, providing a clear audit trail for future reviews.

Type of Journal Entry

Description

S/4HANA AI Automation

Standard Entries

Monthly accruals for expenses like utilities or salaries.

S/4HANA uses AI to predict and automate recurring standard entries based on historical data, improving efficiency and accuracy.

Recurring Entries

Lease payments or subscription fees that occur regularly.

AI algorithms in S/4HANA identify patterns and automate the setup and posting of recurring entries, reducing manual intervention.

Adjusting Entries

Year-end adjustments for prepaid expenses or inventory changes.

S/4HANA's AI capabilities assist in suggesting necessary adjustments and automate postings based on predefined rules and criteria.

Accrual Entries

Accruing unpaid invoices for services received but not yet billed.

AI in S/4HANA predicts and accrues expenses and revenues, ensuring timely recognition and minimal manual oversight.

Reclassification Entries

Reclassifying expenses from one department to another.

AI tools help automate reclassifications by analyzing transaction data and suggesting necessary reallocations.

Tax-related Entries

Journal entries related to tax provisions or deferred tax liabilities.

S/4HANA utilizes AI to ensure tax compliance by automating complex calculations and adjustment entries according to tax laws.

Adjustments for Errors

Correcting a previously recorded entry due to misclassification or numerical error.

AI-driven error detection and correction features in S/4HANA help identify discrepancies and propose corrections efficiently.

 

In conclusion, implementing an AI-powered validation system for parked manual journal entries will streamline the accounting process, reduce errors, ensure compliance, and save significant time and effort during month-end, quarter-end, and year-end closing periods.

Srinath Ganesan

entry.title: SAP Developer News, December 5th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/12/05/sap-developer-news-december-5th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Orchestration service of Generative AI Hub

  • Learning Journey on Generative AI Hub: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-your-business-problems-using-prompts-and-llms-in-sap-s-generative-ai-hub/using-generative-ai-hub-sdk-to-leverage-power-of-llms
  • SAP Help Orchestration service of Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/orchestration-workflow

New UI for SAP Build Apps

  • Daniel’s blog on new UI: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/review-new-layout-features-for-sap-build-apps/ba-p/13933240

Updated integration architecture guide and API Management

  • Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/integration-architecture-guide-updated-version-available-now/ba-p/13930016
  • Please share your experience - new API Management survey: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/please-share-your-experience-new-api-management-survey/ba-p/13950848

karma-ui5 plugin deprecation and upcoming UI5 & CAP events

  • karma-ui5 deprecation blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deprecation-of-karma-ui5-plugin/ba-p/13954060
  • UI5ers Live 12 December https://www.linkedin.com/events/ui5erslive-december20247268259526246645760/about/
  • CAP Customer Roundtable 12 December https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-application-programming-model-eighth-customer-roundtable/ba-p/13951006

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Orchestration service of Generative AI Hub

1:03 New UI for SAP Build Apps

2:10 Updated integration architecture guide and API Management

3:00 karma-ui5 plugin deprecation and upcoming UI5 & CAP events

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for December 5th, 2024.

[Nora] There's a lot of talk about the integration of Microsoft Co-pilot and Joule here at UKISUG, but if you want to try out and build your own customized AI use cases, then you should try out Generative AI Hub. We had a session on it on Sunday, where we actually used the orchestration service and the JavaScript SDK and the Python SDK to build our own AI use cases. And what I love most about the orchestration service is that you can use the same code base for all the available models that we have, and you just have to change the model name parameter to try out the different models and compare the results which I think is awesome. We also have a lot of other features in the orchestration service for example the content filtering and the data masking the prompt templating and obviously the grounding feature which you need if you want to build your own RAG use cases and the grounding feature is already available in the API in the next coming week it's also going to be available via the SDKs and I'm super excited to try it out myself let me know what you think once you try it out.

[Daniel] SAP Build Apps updated its design time UI to put front and center the things that you use most often and to hide the things that you use less often or even just once during your project development. So the first thing they did is to update the main menu. So you have just the main parts of building an application, which is the UI, the variables, which I consider part of the logic, and connecting to data resources. Then they moved the preview and build or now publish buttons in the upper right in a place where most IDEs would put them. And finally, they created a much easier way to handle managing pages, creating new pages or navigating to those pages. And that way you don't have to go into the page UI. I've written a blog that gives a review of all the changes, some tips on using them or finding what you need, though, and also my opinion on some of the features and perhaps some of the features I would like to see in the future. So check out the blog, and I'll put the link in the description.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. We're here at the UKISUG Connect event in Birmingham, and I have some quick leads to share with you. First, the Integration Architecture Guide was recently updated. It is structured according to the concepts of the Integration Solution Advisor Methodology. And it provides you guidelines about all things integration, including process integration, event-driven integration, API integrations, data integration, and user integration. So make sure to check it out. Also, we want your feedback regarding the API management capability. There is a new server available and we want you to learn more about how you're implementing and benefiting from the capability, or even why you're not using API management. So, we're looking forward to hearing back from you.

[DJ] I've just got time for some news items on count. And UI5, first UI5. As some of you already may be aware, the Karma UI5 plugin is deprecated. This follows the deprecation of the Karma test runner itself upon which the plugin is based. But don't despair, there are plenty of other testing options available, including the UI5 test runner. And also, there's an event next week on Thursday the 12th of December, UI5ers Live, where this deprecation is discussed along with alternative options. And talking of next week, Thursday the 12th of December, there's a second event that day for CAP developers. It's another CAP customer round table with lots of news and updates from the CAP team and discussion of topics from the community. That's you. So check out Oliver's blog post on the deprecation and also the event pages. Links, as always, are in the description.

entry.title: On-Premise AI Excellence: How SAP HANA EE and Skybuffer AI Deliver
entry.content:
All right, AI stories are nice and interesting; however, as SAP posted this week, “Today’s geopolitical environment is defined by uncertainty.” This is moving us toward the necessity of controlling innovative AI solutions in disconnected-from-Internet cases.

Can an Enterprise-Level AI Solution Run In Your Datacenter?
Of course, no problem at all! Let’s think a bit—there is an awesome SAP HANA Enterprise Edition available that has an embedded applications layer, and, in fact, this is a Cloud Foundry solution with all necessary enterprise security mechanisms, all delivered by SAP.

Fig. 1 Skybuffer AI Solution Architecture 

Great! So, we have the following on-premise ingredients:

  • SAP Cloud Foundry Cockpit
  • SAP HANA Database
  • SAP Applications Runtime
  • SAP Job Scheduling

These ingredients allow us to develop SAP Fiori applications using XSA technology directly on the SAP HANA EE application layer.

What is missing?
In a general sense, a few things are missing in SAP HANA EE on-premise to act as your enterprise AI on-premise platform:

  • SAP HANA Vector Engine for document information processing (RAG scenarios)
  • SAP AI Core
  • Action Server (no-code SAP Fiori applications allowing AI modeling)

Is the on-premise gap closed?
Yes! Starting in September 2024, our Skybuffer AI partner solution, developed on SAP technologies, successfully passed the pilot stage and is now available in the SAP Store in both versions:

  • SAP BTP Deployment
  • SAP HANA EE (on-premise) Deployment

We have brought the following missing parts to SAP HANA EE on-premise:

  • SAP HANA Partner Vector Engine
  • AI Core (de facto your preferred on-premise LLM model, for example, Llama 3.1 8B, running inside the SAP Applications Layer in Docker)
  • A set of SAP Fiori applications for no-code AI modeling

 Fig. 2 Skybuffer AI Modeling Dashboard

As a result, you get a ready-to-use platform that integrates Generative AI, RAG, and Conversational AI, allowing businesses to automate tasks quickly and efficiently. It saves costs, reduces development complexity, and accelerates time to value. Everything is on-premise on SAP HANA EE (which you can host even under the table 🙂 ).

Cherry on the cake!
Skybuffer AI is out-of-the-box connected to SAP ECC and SAP S4HANA on-premise systems. We have already developed approximately 400 different integrated business scenarios that allow you to use secure AI assistants connected to your SAP ECC and SAP S4HANA via single sign-on. Yes, another magic—no need to adjust roles and profiles for your users; they get in chat exactly the same information they are authorized to see in the SAP system.

Fig. 3 SAP ECC or SAP S4HANA in Microsoft Teams Channel

What channels are covered?
Communication can be done through many channels, the most popular are:

  • Microsoft Teams
  • Web Chat component (can be embedded in any web page)
  • Zoom
  • SAP Fiori Web Chat

Everything is just a configuration on the Skybuffer AI platform (on your premises)—no need for Python, Node.js, or any other type of development.

What about AI Agents?
Sure, those nice 24/7 employees are also here! Any AI model from Skybuffer AI can be scheduled to execute monitoring, document processing, or any other task. Each time an AI Agent runs, you see the execution results in the conversation log because the AI Agent uses generative AI to explain what has been done and tag the log.

 Fig. 4 AI Agent Generative Log

You can chat with the AI Agent anytime to check the heartbeat of the process. Is it not magic?

Fig. 5 AI Agents Status Check

What about RAG document processing?
Here, we not only supply an RAG solution that is ready to consume without any single line of code. Skybuffer AI also has an RAG Document Viewer that allows your employees to see the documents currently accessible by RAG, check document content, and upload new documents directly from the desktop.

 Fig. 6 Skybuffer AI - RAG Document Management

All documents have metadata parameters, so you can classify documents using 48 out-of-the-box string, numbering, and date-type parameters. What you need to do is define your aliases for parameters, so the RAG Fiori application will speak your company’s terms.

What about Joule?
Let’s wait for Q1 2025. Once SAP makes Joule Build available for SAP Partners, we will connect the Skybuffer AI platform to be available via the Joule communication channel as well. In other words, we complement Joule by bringing Skybuffer AI skills directly into Joule’s embedded AI skills.

Interesting and exciting? Agree!

What do you need to run enterprise-level AI on-premise?
Only SAP HANA, which you most probably already have!

Fig. 7 Skybuffer AI - Action Server for no-code AI Modeling

Performance?
Hmmm, that is something really cool. Old, nice on-premise performance, finally 🙂

entry.title: Document Information Extraction Free Tier Now Includes Premium Edition Capabilities
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce an exciting upgrade to the Free Tier service plan for Document Information Extraction! As of now, the Free Tier option on SAP BTP offers the advanced capabilities of the Premium Edition plan. This includes access to cutting-edge Generative AI features driven by the latest advancements in the space of Large Language Models (LLMs).

Enhanced Capabilities Now Available

The integration of the Premium Edition capabilities into the Free Tier plan means that you can now leverage all the powerful features such as:

- Auto Extraction without model training: Automatically extract business-relevant entities from documents without the need for extensive model training.

- Instant Learning Based on User Corrections: The platform now learns and improves in real-time based on user inputs and corrections.

- Embedded Document Classification: Classify documents seamlessly with embedded classification capabilities.

These features enable businesses to automate and accelerate document processing, improving efficiency and accuracy without additional costs.

Getting Started with the Enhanced Free Tier

To start using the upgraded Free Tier service plan for Document Information Extraction, follow these steps:

  1. Register and create an account in SAP BTP: This account will grant you access to the Document Information Extraction service with the Premium Edition capabilities.
  2. Explore the Free Tier Quota: The free tier offers a quota of 50 uploaded document pages per tenant within a rolling period of 30 days. Make the most of this allocation to test and build your scenarios.

Tutorials to Get Enabled

We have prepared tutorials to help you set up and utilize the Free Tier plan of the Document Information Extraction service:

- Get an Account on SAP BTP to Try Out Free Tier Service Plans: Step-by-step instructions to register and create your SAP BTP account.

- Use Free Tier to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application: A guide to setting up the Document Information Extraction service and accessing the application.

These resources will empower you to fully test and explore the capabilities of the Premium Edition within the Free Tier plan. 

When you’re ready to take the next step, transitioning from the Free Tier to productive usage is seamless. To make this transition even more appealing, we’ve introduced a new pricing structure with multiple tiers. For more details about this updated pricing model, be sure to check out this article article.    

Conclusion

This upgrade is a testament to our commitment to providing high-value tools and services accessible to all businesses. By integrating Generative AI capabilities into the Free Tier plan, we aim to democratize access to advanced document processing technologies, enabling you to harness the full potential of SAP AI Services.

entry.title: Unlocking Value with New Tiers and Pricing for Document Information Extraction Premium Edition
entry.content:
We hope this message finds you well. We have some exciting updates to share regarding our service that are designed to enhance your experience and provide even greater value.

Price Adjustment and New Tiering Concept

As part of our ongoing commitment to delivering cost-effective solutions, we have introduced a new pricing structure along with a tiering concept for our premium edition of the service. This change is particularly beneficial for high-volume use cases, offering more attractive pricing options for our valued customers.

The new pricing structure applies to the service plan: premium_edition. The metric is blocks of 100 documents per month:

- Up to 5 blocks (500 documents)

- Up to 500 blocks (50,000 documents)

- Up to 1,000 blocks (100,000 documents)

- From 1,000 blocks onwards (100,000+ documents)

These new tiers make it easier for you to leverage the cutting-edge large language model technology in a secure and reliable manner, adhering to SAP product, security, and DPP standards at more favorable pricing. Please check the price updates in SAP Discovery Center.

We also want to inform you that as of this month you can also embark on your Premium journey with the 
Free Tier service plan. It allows you to test and explore the service free of charge. You can then easily switch to the productive environment once you're ready. For more details about the enhancements to the Free Tier plan, please refer to this article.

Key Values of Our Service

We'd also like to remind you of the key benefits our Document Information Extraction service offers:

- Auto Extraction without Model Training: Automatically extract business-relevant entities from documents without the need for extensive model training.

- Instant Learning Based on User Corrections: Our platform improves in real-time based on your inputs and corrections, ensuring that the service evolves to meet your specific needs.

- Embedded Document Classification: Experience seamless document classification with our embedded capabilities, making your workflow more efficient.

- Expanded Availability in Data Centers: our service is now available in additional data center regions to enhance accessibility and performance: Azure Netherlands (Amsterdam), Azure U.S. East (Virginia), Azure Australia (Sydney), AWS Southeast Asia (Singapore).

Note: further deployments will follow until the end of 2024: GCP Germany (Frankfurt), GCP U.S. Iowa (Central), GCP Japan East (Tokyo).

We are continuously working to improve our service offerings, and we appreciate your support and the continuous feedback that we are receiving from you.

Thank you for being a valued member of our community. Should you have any questions or require further information, please do not hesitate to reach out.

entry.title: Success factors for custom AI use cases: from the idea to the productive phase
entry.content:
The question of whether or not the use of AI in a company can be a value-added booster has long been answered - in fact, it is almost negligent not to consider the use of AI in one's company. Nevertheless, our experience shows that only a portion of the AI use case ideas considered for implementation ultimately makes it to the productive phase. At SAP, we understand that the introduction of AI use cases is a challenging journey into often unfamiliar territory. This makes it even more important to have an approach that provides clear orientation.

A lifecycle stage model for categorizing AI use cases is a critical tool for navigating the challenges of AI adoption and ensuring successful implementation. It offers several advantages: Many initiatives fail due to technical difficulties, unclear goals, or a lack of user acceptance. By applying a staged approach, resources can be allocated more effectively, risks identified early, and focus directed toward promising projects. This ensures that AI use cases are efficiently developed, from the initial idea to impactful deployment. Additionally, it serves as an excellent methodology for monitoring whether there are sufficient ideas and use cases in the early stages to meet company KPIs, such as achieving a specific number of go-live use cases each year.

These insights are based on experience within a successful AI program at a client and are specifically true for custom AI use cases.
